The '''HK-77 assassin droid''' was an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] developed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], but they were not produced or deployed until the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:HK-77.jpg|thumb|left|180px|HK-77 assassin droid]]
The HK-77 assassin droid was considered the pinnacle of the [[HK-series assassin droid]] lines that were designed by the Separatists. It was based on the [[HK-47]], [[HK-57]], [[HK-58 Aurek|HK-58]], and [[HK-67]] models. It was slightly shorter than the other models, and had a narrow head with optical sensors that extended from each side of it. Unlike the earlier models, the HK-77 had blaster rifles integrated into its arms for quick and easy access. HK-77s came in 2 primary variants: assault droids and squad leaders.

It was considered extremely aggressive and preferred overt action to stealth. It would often reveal itself to a target and give it an opportunity to escape or attack first.<ref name="SGtD" />

It cost 19,865 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. A popular modification was to convert the HK-77 into a firefighting droid by replacing the blaster rifles with fire extinguishers and coating the armor with a heat-resistant material.<ref name="SGtD" />

==History==
The HK-77 assault droid was designed by Separatist engineers after finding and reverse engineering HK-47, attempting to combine the assassin droid's design with their battle droids. Unlike their predecessor, these droids are made for more direct combat. HK-77s were designed to serve as bodyguards or henchmen to larger droids. A small army of HK-77 units was created by [[HK-47]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] during the Galactic Civil War, but were defeated by a group of spacers.<ref name="Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan]]''</ref> Several made it off world and continued their function as assassins, but others were repurposed for various jobs, including firefighters and gamblers.<ref name="SGtD" />
